title:
Lesson 1:
Don't Worry / Everything Will Be Fine
description: 'No te preocupes' = Don't worry. 'Todo va a estar bien' = Everything will be fine. 'Espero que estés bien' = I hope you're doing well. These are comforting phrases for a friend in need.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.spanish: Estoy muy preocupada por el examen.
dialogue.english: I'm very worried about the exam.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.spanish: No te preocupes. Has estudiado mucho.
dialogue.english: Don't worry. You've studied a lot.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.spanish: Pero y si no lo paso...
dialogue.english: But what if I don't pass...

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.spanish: Todo va a estar bien. Confía en ti.
dialogue.english: Everything will be fine. Trust yourself.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.spanish: Gracias. Espero que estés bien tú también.
dialogue.english: Thank you. I hope you're doing well too.

title:
Lesson 2:
Get Well Soon
description: 'Espero que te mejores' = I hope you get better. 'Que te mejores' = Get well soon (shorter, warmer form). Both are used when someone is sick. You can also say these after bad news.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.spanish: No puedo salir hoy. Estoy enferma.
dialogue.english: I can't go out today. I'm sick.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: Oh, I'm so sorry. What's wrong?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.spanish: Espero que te mejores pronto. Descansa.
dialogue.english: I hope you get better soon. Rest up.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.spanish: Que te mejores. Avísame si necesitas algo.
dialogue.english: Get well soon. Let me know if you need anything.
